title: Why Government Wants To Ban Milk Importation By 2022
description: Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development, Sabo Nanono, has said the Federal Government will ban milk importation by 2022. Nanono made the disclosure while speaking to journalists in Abuja, at an
html:
Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development, Sabo Nanono, has said the Federal Government will ban milk importation by 2022. Nanono made the disclosure while speaking to journalists in Abuja, at an event to commemorate 2020 World Food Day.

He explained that the ministry was making frantic efforts to ensure the availability of resources for milk and dairy production.

“We are planning in this ministry and watch my words, in the next two years we will ban the importation of milk into this country. And ask me why: we have 25 million cows in this country to produce five million litres per day” he said.

“The issue is now logistics, which we have started by setting up milk processing plants across the country. I see no reason why we should import milk in the next two years. We should stop importation of milk”

Nanono also said Nigeria is currently the largest producer of cassava in the world. He said, “Nigeria has curbed imports and has established a robust rice production programme to encourage more rice production at home.”

The minister further stated that the prices of food commodities which have currently gone up because of the COVID-19 pandemic would drop in a couple of months.
